_meta.author: open-wildguard-hub contributors
_meta.note: English locale. All keys must mirror locales/it.json exactly.

alert_dispatch_msg: Quick response patrol dispatched to sensor: {{sensor}} ({{station}}).
map_route_msg: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal MST) patrol route generated connecting all forest stations.
module_1_desc: Central FastAPI & SQLite API orchestrating physical nodes and OSINT data. Implements store-and-forward logic for offline scenarios.
module_2_desc: Image classification on camera traps and drones via YOLOv8. Uses OpenCV edge filters to drop wind/leaf alerts on the microcamera.
module_3_desc: Low-power microphone network tracking gunshots and chainsaws. Uses Time Difference of Arrival (TDoA) to calculate shooter coordinates.
module_4_desc: Georeferenced 3D maps based on GIS, GeoJSON, and PyDeck to highlight patrol pathways and wildlife migration corridors.
module_5_desc: Biometric and IoT tracking of collar devices on protected wildlife, triggering real-time geo-fencing alerts.
module_6_desc: Offline-first mobile application for rangers and communities to log tracks and sightings via periodic base syncs.
module_7_desc: Ethical scraping of illicit black markets, Telegram, and news across 7 languages. Automatic entities extraction using mDeBERTa NLP.
module_8_desc: Integrating PAWS predictive engine and DDPG Reinforcement Learning to generate unpredictable, optimal patrol routes.
module_9_desc: Certifying media evidence using immutable SHA-256 hash chains and auto-generating court chargesheets linked to legal schedules.
module_10_desc: Collaboratively train YOLOv8 across sanctuaries without sharing raw geolocated images, protecting wildlife data sovereignty.
docs_arduino_desc: The camouflaged, low-cost ground nodes are based on Arduino Nicla Vision or ESP32-CAM. They use a PIR sensor to detect thermal presence/movement, wake the microcontroller from deep-sleep, capture a frame, and execute edge inference via TinyML (TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers), filtering out foliage/wind variations before satellite or LoRa transmission.
docs_repos_desc: The open-wildguard-hub framework acts as a central orchestrator, integrating and reusing specific code from the 16 downloaded repositories. Below are the reference directories and how code integrates to implement the planned features:
proposal_title: Institutional Pitch for WWF, Reserves & Forestry Guards
proposal_desc: open-wildguard-hub is designed as a low-cost, sustainable ecosystem for NGOs and government agencies. It ensures legal custody of evidence, optimizes patrol coordination with stochastic game theory, and reduces edge camera false alarms by 90%.
proposal_benefit_1_desc: Fully open-source codebase running on off-the-shelf standard hardware (Arduino/ESP32). No subscription plans, recurring maintenance fees, or proprietary technology vendor lock-ins.
proposal_benefit_2_desc: All digital evidence gets hashed (SHA-256) on capture and bound to local immutable hash chains. This guarantees data integrity and refutes legal challenges on camera trap photos in court.
proposal_benefit_3_desc: Edge nodes disable radio modules immediately after transmitting an event (anti-tagging). This prevents radio-equipped poachers from scanning and locating ranger squads or watchtowers.
